about.label: Profile
about.title: I design the product and direct its build; AI implements, I decide.
about.paragraph1: Starting from a problem, I design the architecture, write the specifications, and supervise implementation with AI agents. I work the product end to end: backend, frontend, deployment, and infrastructure. I don't delegate judgment: I decide where complexity is worth it and review what the AI produces.
about.paragraph2: Some of this software runs in production with real clients. The rest are products of my own and tools I build to solve concrete problems and use daily. Security is part of the process from the start, not a final add-on.

details.stackValue: FastAPI · React · PostgreSQL/MySQL · Redis · Celery · Docker

skills.label: Stack
skills.title: What I work with.
skills.line: Backend with Python (FastAPI, Flask), async tasks with Celery and Redis, data in PostgreSQL and MySQL. Frontend with React, Vite, and Tailwind. Deployment with Docker and Nginx on VPS. Applied AI with Gemini models and agent patterns. Security (auth, encryption, hardening) built in from the design stage.

projects.label: Featured projects
projects.title: Software in production and tools of my own.
projects.intro: Some run in production with real clients; others are products and tools I built and use daily. I present them from the perspective of product, architecture, and technical decisions.

micartaya.title: MiCartaYa
micartaya.description: Digital menu and ordering SaaS for restaurants: QR menu, mobile ordering, real-time management, and direct kitchen printing.

bullets.first: "Reels" video mode for products, a paid add-on (+€25/month) generating direct product revenue.
bullets.second: AI chatbot (SmartWaiter) that recommends dishes on the public menu, opt-in per restaurant.
bullets.third: Automatic menu translation with AI (Gemini, falling back to Google Translate without an API key).

micartaya.directAccess: → MiCartaYa – Digital menu and ordering for restaurants.

modal.title: Digital menu and real-time ordering for 14 restaurants.
modal.paragraph1: MiCartaYa digitizes the menu and ordering in the restaurant. The diner scans a QR code, orders from their phone, and the order reaches the kitchen in real time with direct ticket printing. It's in production with 14 restaurants.
modal.paragraph2: Beyond operations, the product carries applied AI: a chatbot (SmartWaiter) that recommends dishes on the public menu, opt-in per restaurant; automatic menu translation with Gemini (falling back to Google Translate); and a 'Reels' video mode for products that is its own paid add-on with recurring revenue.

bullets.first: WebSocket printer client: the ticket reaches the kitchen via instant push, no polling.
bullets.second: Strict separation between order status and payment status: trusts only the Dojo webhook, never the browser redirect.
bullets.third: Multi-tenant by subdomain, third-party credentials encrypted per restaurant, and POS sync (DST).

modal.next: Next iteration: at-table payments and a loyalty program.

orderya.title: OrderYa
orderya.description: Centralized ordering system between bakery and shops for hospitality, with order prediction and per-product profitability.

bullets.first: Automatically calculates whether each order is profitable: per-product costs and margins, with history.
bullets.second: Order prediction engine to anticipate demand before it arrives.
bullets.third: Multi-tenant with company-to-company isolation, POS integration (DST), and 4-role access control.

orderya.directAccess: → OrderYa – Orders between bakery and shops for hospitality.

modal.title: Orders between bakery and shops, with prediction and profitability.
modal.paragraph1: OrderYa centralizes ordering between a bakery and its points of sale. It replaces WhatsApp and spreadsheets with a single flow where each shop orders and the bakery sees consolidated demand. In production for one restaurant and one bakery.
modal.paragraph2: Beyond CRUD, it carries real business logic: an order prediction engine, a profitability module that computes per-product costs and margins with snapshots, and minimum stock per shop. Company isolation is strict, multi-tenant by subdomain.

bullets.first: Order prediction and monthly close with per-supplier splits.
bullets.second: Per-product profitability with costs, margins, and historical snapshots.
bullets.third: Article sync with POS (DST) and export to PDF/Excel/barcode.

modal.next: Next iteration: refine prediction with historical data and holiday calendars.

ponme.title: Ponme
ponme.description: An app that builds your outfit in under a minute from your own digitized wardrobe, combining clothes with AI.

bullets.first: Before you buy new clothes, it tells you if they're worth it with what you already own (smart purchase check, PRO).
bullets.second: Analyzes each garment with AI (color, style, season) and generates outfits based on weather and occasion.
bullets.third: Learns your style profile over time and packs your suitcase for trips.

modal.title: Multimodal AI to solve 'what do I wear' in under a minute.
modal.paragraph1: Ponme digitizes your wardrobe and builds outfits combining clothes with AI. When you upload a garment, a multimodal pipeline analyzes it (color, style, season) and catalogs it on its own; then the generator proposes combinations based on weather and occasion. It's in production, with Stripe billing and real users.
modal.paragraph2: Beyond generating outfits, it includes a smart purchase check (does this garment pay off with what I already own?), weekly capsules and a trip packing planner, a taste profile that learns from your choices, and a public outfit feed with shareable links that works as a built-in distribution channel.

bullets.first: Real-AI vs demo gate to control cost per unverified user.
bullets.second: Smart purchase check and a taste profile learned over time (both PRO).
bullets.third: Public outfit feed with shareable links, designed as its own distribution channel.

modal.next: Current focus: getting the product into more hands.

beatlist.tech: Flask · OAuth 2.0 · Celery · Spotify API · Cohere
beatlist.title: BeatList
beatlist.description: A web app that connects to Spotify to manage playlists and generate musical-profile analysis, with real users in several countries.

bullets.first: Official Spotify API integration via OAuth 2.0, approved by Spotify.
bullets.second: Musical-profile analysis with AI (Cohere), with a decoupleable queue (ThreadPool or Celery by load).
bullets.third: Cache with snapshot-based invalidation and worker auto-tuning by CPU and concurrency.

beatlist.directAccess: → BeatList – Spotify analytics approved by Spotify.

modal.title: Spotify playlist analytics and management, with users in several countries.
modal.paragraph1: BeatList connects to the Spotify API to go beyond the standard player: it manages playlists (create, merge, reorder) and generates musical-profile analysis. It uses official OAuth 2.0 authentication, approved by Spotify, and has real users in several countries.
modal.paragraph2: Heavy analysis doesn't block the interface: it's delegated to a queue that can run on ThreadPool or Celery depending on expected load. The cache is invalidated by snapshot to avoid recomputing, and Gunicorn workers auto-tune by CPU and concurrency.

bullets.first: Advanced playlist management and listening-habit analysis.
bullets.second: AI analysis (Cohere) with rate-limit and demo mode.
bullets.third: CI with auto-patching of vulnerabilities (pip-audit) and i18n.

modal.next: Next iteration: collaborative mode and advanced exports.

orbe.tech: React · TypeScript · Express · Gemini · Yahoo Finance
orbe.title: Orbe
orbe.description: A financial-intelligence panel I built to understand my own portfolio: quotes, news, and AI analysis in one place.

bullets.first: Aggregates multiple sources (quotes, news, calendar, indicators) over real portfolio data.
bullets.second: AI layer (Gemini) for qualitative analysis: biases, risk audit, and briefing.
bullets.third: Automated weekly briefing by email.

modal.title: Understanding the market without living glued to it.
modal.paragraph1: Orbe is a tool I built and use to run my own portfolio. The idea: understand why a stock moves and what's going on, without having to be an expert or follow the market all day. It aggregates quotes, news, calendar, and indicators in a single panel.
modal.paragraph2: What makes it different from just another dashboard isn't the stack, it's the analysis layer: an AI integration that interprets the real portfolio data qualitatively (psychological biases, risk audit, briefing) and sends a weekly summary by email. It's single-user, me, by design.

bullets.first: Multi-source aggregation over real portfolio data.
bullets.second: Qualitative AI analysis and bias detection.
bullets.third: Automated weekly briefing by email.

modal.next: Personal-use tool; it evolves with what I need.

dedica.title: Dedica
dedica.description: A time and expense tracking app by project that exposes its entire API as an MCP server, so an AI agent can operate it directly.

bullets.first: Entire API exposed as an MCP server (16 tools: projects, time entries, expenses...).
bullets.second: Own OAuth 2.0 authorization server to authenticate MCP access over HTTP.
bullets.third: Lets an agent create time entries, expenses, and projects without going through the UI.

modal.title: Time tracking designed to be operated by an agent, not just a person.
modal.paragraph1: Dedica logs hours and expenses by project and computes whether a project is profitable. I built it for personal use, but the interesting part isn't the tracking: it's that its entire API is exposed as an MCP server, with 16 tools, for an AI agent to operate directly.
modal.paragraph2: For that I set up my own OAuth 2.0 authorization server that authenticates MCP access over HTTP. In practice, a Claude agent can create a time entry, log an expense, or open a project without touching the interface. It's the piece that best sums up how I work: I design the system so the AI can operate within it.

bullets.first: 16 MCP tools over the app's full API.
bullets.second: Own OAuth 2.0 for agent access over HTTP.
bullets.third: Time tracking, expenses, and profitability by project and work type.

modal.next: Personal-use tool; a base for experimenting with agents operating real software.

yasolutions.title: YaSolutions
yasolutions.description: The central website for FirmaYa, PresupuestoYa, and every product in the Ya family.

others.trato: Multi-tenant CRM with 2FA, automated follow-ups, and rate-limited API keys for integrations. It connects to PresupuestoYa through SSO and syncs clients, products, and quotes in real time.
others.firmaya: Internal simple electronic-signature platform: visual PDF field editor, handwritten signing from mobile or browser, and a complete evidence trail with hashes and audit history.
others.presupuestoya: Multi-tenant SaaS for creating and presenting interactive sales quotes, with a multimedia catalog, online acceptance, auditing, and controlled SSO integration with Trato.
others.kobra: Internal billing and collections platform: automates alerts, reminders, and exports with traceable cronjobs.
others.mesasya: Reservation and table-management platform for restaurants, with real-time availability via SSE (Flask).
others.tpvpanel: Panel + agent that inventories and remotely monitors a fleet of POS systems (hardware, OS, versions) and maps their location.
others.tuparte: Personal finance app: a rules engine that analyzes habits and splits each income across spending, savings, and investment.
